Now I have a clear view of atom 20 (C) and its covalent bonds. Based on my visual inspection from multiple angles, here are my findings:

## Neighbour Atoms of Atom 20 (C)

Atom 20 (C) is covalently bonded to the following **3 neighbour atoms**:

| Atomic Index | Element |
|---|---|
| 19 | N (Nitrogen) |
| 21 | N (Nitrogen) |
| 29 | C (Carbon) |

**Explanation:** Atom C:20 is part of what appears to be a heterocyclic/aromatic system. It is connected to:
- **N:19** — a nitrogen atom on one side
- **N:21** — a nitrogen atom on the other side
- **C:29** — a carbon atom below it

These three bonds are consistent with a carbon atom in an aromatic ring bridging two nitrogen atoms.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 20, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
